title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Kristen Redfern orchestrates a complex deception to frame Linda Marshall for the murder of Elena Marshall. To create an alibi and manipulate time, Kristen tampers with Linda’s watch, setting it twenty minutes fast. She also applies brown sunscreen to her body to disguise her skin tone and later throws the empty bottle out of a window. To frame Kenneth Marshall, the conspirators had previously broken one of his pipes near Elven Bay.

During the execution of the plan, Kristen uses a white swimsuit and loose clothing to hide her tanned skin. After resetting Linda's watch to the correct time, Kristen climbs a cliff, hides her clothes, and uses a green hat with a red wig to pose as a corpse on the beach. When Patrick Redfern and Emily Brust arrive by boat, Patrick discovers the "body." Fearing the killer is nearby, Emily rows back to the bay to find the police, leaving Patrick alone. Kristen then destroys the hat and wig, hiding the pieces in her swimsuit before returning to the hotel to wash off the sunscreen and change into a tennis outfit. She burns the remains of the hat in Linda's fireplace alongside a calendar page to make it appear as though Linda was performing a witchcraft spell.

While the deception unfolds, Patrick Redfern goes to the fairy hole and kills Elena Marshall by clutching her neck. Hercule Poirot explains to Rosamond Dunnley that while Patrick had a motive to steal Elena's money, his presence on the beach provided an alibi. Poirot concludes that Kristen and Patrick intended to make the child, Linda, a scapegoat by exploiting her belief in magic and her guilt over a ritual she performed.
